title: Values and Priorities List In my own Wise Mind, I believe it is important to:  Вү A. Attend to relationships.
source_marker: Emotion Regulation Handout 18 (Emotion Regulation Worksheets 10, 12, 13) (p. 1 of 3)

pages.text:
253
From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan. Permission
to photocopy this handout is granted to purchasers of this book for personal use only (see copyright page for details).
вҳҗ
Emotion Regulation Handout 18 (Emotion Regulation Worksheets 10, 12, 13) (p. 1 of 3)
Values and Priorities List
In my own Wise Mind, I believe it is important to:

Вү A. Attend to relationships. 1. пҒұ	Repair old relationships. 2.	пҒұ	Reach out for new relationships. 3.	пҒұ	Work on current relationships. 4.	пҒұ	End destructive relationships. пҒұ	Other:

Вү B. Be part of a group. 5.	пҒұ	Have close and satisfying relationships with others. 6.	пҒұ	Feel a sense of belonging. 7. пҒұ	Receive affection and love. 8.	пҒұ	Be involved and intimate with others; have and keep close friends. 9.	пҒұ	Have a family; stay close to and spend time with family members.
10. пҒұ	Have people to do things with. пҒұ	Other:

Вү C. Be powerful and able to influence others.
11. пҒұ	Have the authority to approve or disapprove of what people do, or to control how
resources are used.
12. пҒұ	Be a leader.
13. пҒұ	Make a great deal of money.
14. пҒұ	Be respected by others.
15. пҒұ	Be seen by others as successful; become well known; obtain recognition and status.
16. пҒұ	Compete successfully with others.
17. пҒұ	Be popular and accepted. пҒұ	Other:

Вү D. Achieve things in life.
18. пҒұ	Achieve significant goals; be involved in undertakings I believe are significant.
19. пҒұ	Be productive.
20.	пҒұ	Work toward goals; work hard.
21. пҒұ	Be ambitious. пҒұ	Other:
(continued on next page)
Adapted from Schwartz, S. H. (1992). Universals in the content and structure of values: Theory and empirical tests in 20 countries. In M.
Zanna (Ed.), Advances in experimental social psychology (Vol. 25, pp.В 1вҖ“65). New York: Academic Press. Copyright 1992 by Academic Press.
Adapted by permission of Elsevier B.V.

254
Emotion Regulation Handout 18 (p. 2 of 3)

Вү E. Live a life of pleasure and satisfaction.
22.	пҒұ	Have a good time.
23.	пҒұ	Seek fun and things that give pleasure.
24.	пҒұ	Have free time.
25.	пҒұ	Enjoy the work I do. пҒұ	Other:

Вү F. Keep life full of exciting events, relationships, and things.
26.	пҒұ	Try new and different things in life.
27. пҒұ	Be daring and seek adventures.
28.	пҒұ	Have an exciting life. пҒұ	Other:

Вү G. Behave respectfully.
29.	пҒұ	Be humble and modest; do not draw attention to myself.
30.	пҒұ	Follow traditions and customs; behave properly.
31. пҒұ	Do what I am told and follow rules.
32.	пҒұ	Treat others well. пҒұ	Other:

Вү H. Be self-directed.
33.	пҒұ	Follow my own path in life.
34.	пҒұ	Be innovative, think of new ideas, and be creative.
35.	пҒұ	Make my own decisions and be free.
36.	пҒұ	Be independent; take care of myself and those I am responsible for.
37. пҒұ	Have freedom of thought and action; be able to act in terms of my own priorities. пҒұ	Other:

Вү I.
Be a spiritual person.
38.	пҒұ	Make room in life for spirituality; live life according to spiritual principles.
39.	пҒұ	Practice a religion or faith.
40.	пҒұ	Grow in understanding of myself, my personal calling, and lifeвҖҷs real purpose.
41. пҒұ	Discern and do the will of God (or a higher power) and find lasting meaning in life. пҒұ	Other:

Вү J. Be secure.
42.	пҒұ	Live in secure and safe surroundings.
43.	пҒұ	Be physically healthy and fit.
44.	пҒұ	Have a steady income that meets my own and my familyвҖҷs basic needs. пҒұ	Other:
(continued on next page)

255
Emotion Regulation Handout 18 (p. 3 of 3)

Вү K. Recognize the universal good of all things.
45.	пҒұ	Be fair, treat people equally, and provide equal opportunities.
46.	пҒұ	Understand different people; be open-minded.
47. пҒұ	Care for nature and the environment. пҒұ	Other:

Вү L. Contribute to the larger community.
48.	пҒұ	Help people and those in need; care for othersвҖҷ well-being; improve society.
49.	пҒұ	Be loyal to friends and devoted to close people; be committed to a group that shares
my beliefs, values, and ethical principles.
50.	пҒұ	Be committed to a cause or to a group that has a larger purpose beyond my own.
51. пҒұ	Make sacrifices for others. пҒұ	Other:

Вү M. Work at self-development.
52.	пҒұ	Develop a personal philosophy of life.
53.	пҒұ	Learn and do challenging things that help me grow and mature as a human being. пҒұ	Other:

Вү N. Have integrity.
54.	пҒұ	Be honest, and acknowledge and stand up for my personal beliefs.
55.	пҒұ	Be a responsible person; keep my word to others.
56.	пҒұ	Be courageous in facing and living life.
57. пҒұ	Be a person who pays debts to others and repairs damage I have caused.
58.	пҒұ	Be accepting of myself, others, and life as it is; live without resentment. пҒұ	Other:

Вү O. Other:

ourspace_kindness_pass.title: Values and Priorities List In my own Wise Mind, I believe it is important to:  Вү A. Attend to relationships.

main_doability_needs: bed-level or seated options by default
oxygen/breathing, pain, vertigo, fatigue, mobility, and recovery time respected
ADHD/autism supports: transitions, low-stimulation starts, tiny visible steps
partial completion and stopping before a crash count as success
no shame, no body blame, no pressure to push through pain or dysphoria
ADHD transition support before task demands
caregiver burnout recovery treated as care, not avoidance
protected rest, boundaries, decompression, sensory needs, and people-pleasing checks
no assumption that Jasper has unlimited caregiver capacity

household_rules: no hour block may hold more than two tasks/tools
skills may be used as one-breath or one-field versions
survival days load critical care first and skip optional worksheets
rest, boundaries, decompression, and asking for help count as care
skills are for support, not grades

universal_kindness_rules: This is a support tool, not a test.
One line, one checkbox, one breath, or one tiny step counts.
Skipping a skill because the body is unsafe, exhausted, dysregulated, or overloaded is allowed.
Do not stack this with more than one other task inside the same hour.
Survival-day version always wins over perfection.
william_accessibility_adaptations: Use bed-level, seated, voice-note, or caregiver-assisted versions when needed.
Avoid pain escalation, respiratory strain, unsafe transfers, and dysphoria-triggering language.
Break worksheets into one-field chunks; stopping before a crash is success.
jasper_accessibility_adaptations: Start with transition support before expecting focus.
Protect caregiver burnout recovery; rest and boundaries are valid completion.
Use body-doubling, timers, low-stimulation versions, and one-minute starts.
tiny_steps: Name one body sensation or one fact.
Choose one kind next step or stop for rest.

routine_integration.start_the_day_use: eligible as a rotating daily skill, never more than one DBT/ADHD skill in an hour block
routine_integration.recommended_block: after body check or before decompression
